channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: South Africa Mass Shooting: 12 Killed in Johannesburg Informal Settlement
item.description: Multiple attackers opened fire in the Jumpers informal settlement in Cleveland, Johannesburg, killing 12 and wounding 9. Police have launched a manhunt for over 10 suspects.

item.title: Ukraine Signs Drone Deal With Latvia as Zelenskiy Meets Nordic-Baltic Leaders
item.description: Ukraine has signed a drone cooperation agreement with Latvia during a summit with Nordic and Baltic states in Tallinn, Estonia. President Zelenskiy met with Latvian PM Kulbergs to discuss military cooperation.

item.title: Malaysia to Supply Maximum LNG and Naphtha to Japan Under New Agreement
item.description: Malaysia will provide Japan with maximum possible LNG and naphtha supplies as Tokyo seeks to diversify energy sources amid regional instability. The agreement comes as Japan faces energy security concerns.

item.title: Senegal Football Federation Explains Viral Airport Security Footage Before World Cup
item.description: Senegal football federation issued a statement explaining security footage of their World Cup squad undergoing checks on an airport tarmac, after the images prompted accusations of discriminatory treatment.

item.title: Switzerland to Vote on Unprecedented Population Cap at 10 Million
item.description: Swiss voters will decide on a constitutional amendment limiting the population to 10 million, a move that could reshape the country's approach to immigration and sustainability.

item.title: US Students Show First Gains in Reading and Math After Years of Decline
item.description: National test scores show modest improvements for the first time in over a decade, though still below pre-pandemic levels.

item.title: Iran Strikes US Bases in Jordan and Gulf States in Retaliation
item.description: Iran has launched attacks on US military installations in Jordan and Gulf states following US strikes on Iranian targets near the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Poland Demands Seat at Ukraine Peace Negotiations
item.description: Polish PM Donald Tusk insists Poland must be involved in any Ukraine peace negotiations given its strategic position and history.

item.title: Japan Wholesale Inflation Hits 3-Year High on Middle East Energy Shock
item.description: Japanese wholesale inflation surged to 4.9% in May, hitting a three-year high as Middle East energy disruptions drive up import costs.

item.title: Bulgaria's New Government Ends Arms Support for Ukraine
item.description: Bulgaria's newly formed government has announced it will cease providing military aid to Ukraine, reversing previous policy.

item.title: UK Charities Donated £28M to Illegal Israeli Settlements
item.description: An investigation reveals that charities in England and Wales have donated millions to organizations supporting illegal Israeli settlements in the West Bank.

item.title: Taiwan test-fires US-supplied HIMARS toward China in first public display
item.description: Taiwan conducted its first public test of US-supplied HIMARS rocket launchers, firing toward China in a show of defensive capability.

item.title: Protesters torch vehicles and buildings in Belfast after refugee knife attack
item.description: Violent protests erupted in Belfast following a knife attack involving a refugee suspect, with demonstrators setting vehicles and buildings on fire.

item.title: China prepares $295 billion AI infrastructure plan to challenge US dominance
item.description: China is preparing a massive $295 billion investment in AI infrastructure nationwide as part of its strategic competition with the United States.

item.title: Bill Gates to face US Congress questioning over Epstein links
item.description: Bill Gates will testify before Congress regarding his connections to Jeffrey Epstein, amid ongoing scrutiny of high-profile figures.

item.title: IRGC Claims Strikes on US Bases in Jordan, Kuwait and Bahrain
item.description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps claims to have struck US military bases across Jordan, Kuwait, and Bahrain in retaliatory operations.

item.title: Kuwait Intercepts Hostile Aerial Targets Amid Regional Escalation
item.description: Kuwait's air defense systems engaged hostile aerial targets as regional tensions escalate following US strikes on Iran.

item.title: Japan's Largest Banks to Jointly Issue Stablecoins by March 2027
item.description: Three of Japan's largest banks plan to jointly issue stablecoins by March 2027, marking a major development in digital currency adoption.

item.title: Philippines earthquake death toll rises to 45, rescuers search rubble
item.description: The death toll from the powerful 7.8 magnitude earthquake in the southern Philippines has risen to 45, with thousands displaced and rescuers searching for survivors.

item.title: South Korea Kakao workers stage strike demanding job security
item.description: Kakao's unionized workers staged their first-ever strike, demanding changes to bonus systems and job security at South Korea's tech giant.

item.title: Turkey proposes 2035 global electricity target at climate summit
item.description: Climate summit host Turkey proposed a global 2035 electricity target at the COP summit, seeking to accelerate the clean energy transition.

item.title: Peru election remains too close to call as markets rally
item.description: Peru's presidential election remains neck-and-neck with overseas ballots still being counted, while markets rally amid expectations of stability.

item.title: US trade deficit narrows as petroleum exports hit record high
item.description: The US trade deficit narrowed in April as petroleum exports reached record levels, driven by increased energy shipments abroad.

item.title: US Launches Strikes on Iran After Apache Helicopter Downed Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces launched strikes on Iranian targets after an Apache helicopter was downed near the Strait of Hormuz, with Trump confirming the pilots are safe.
